title: 7.5 and 7.6 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lessons 7.5 and 7.6.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does the human body work as a system?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All parts of your body work together to keep you alive.
Each body part functions independently without affecting other parts.
Your heart controls all other body parts.
Your brain works alone and doesn’t interact with your muscles or lungs.
looking at how all parts of something connect and work together
focusing only on one detail of a system
predicting exactly what will happen next
identifying the importance of technology and machines

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are the benefits of combining futuring and systems thinking?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It helps you imagine better futures and solve problems by understanding connections and planning ahead
It lets you predict exactly what will happen in the future.
It helps you focus on just one problem at a time.
It makes problems disappear without needing action.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What types of things should we consider when we build systems of the future?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: We should ask powerful questions, look for loops, imagine future stories, create system maps, and work together as teams.
We should only focus on using advanced technology.
We should avoid working in teams to keep things simple.
We should wait until problems happen, then respond quickly.
the process of envisioning and planning for the future
predicting the exact future
thinking about futuristic technologies
working to avoid any future change

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a valuable step when building systems for the future**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: looking for loops
setting aside questions
avoiding the imagination
working competitively

assessment_items.question_content:
**What scientific law explains why complex structures are less common in the Universe than simpler ones?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The second law of thermodynamics
The law of universal gravitation
Newton's third law of motion
The first law of thermodynamics
a measure of how much disorder and randomness there is in something
the amount of energy in a star
the number of planets in a solar system
the speed of light in space

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why should our Universe be getting less complex?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Because the force of entropy tends to break things down and make them simpler
Because gravity pulls everything together
Because the Universe is shrinking
Because life is disappearing

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does the force of entropy affect complexity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It tends to reduce complexity.
It tends to reduce simplicity.
It tends to create more order.
It does not affect complexity.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the Universe tend toward?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: disorder
life
complexity
contraction

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does collective learning benefit us?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: We can collaborate to avoid disasters and work toward common goals.
We can recover from disasters.
We can act without thinking.
We can ignore other perspectives.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are advantages that collective learning gives us?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Humans can collaborate to solve problems.
Competition fosters innovation.
Learning has gotten more difficult.
We can recover from disasters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**David Christian says that our planet is becoming conscious. What does he mean by this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Humanity is acting like a super-organism.
The planet is developing a brain.
Many species are intelligent.
Evolution is changing the brain.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of humans making conscious decisions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Deciding what to eat for a meal
Breathing through our noses
Repairing damaged tissue in our bodies
Being scared of going bungee jumping
